bird.name: Black-necked stilt
bird.summary: The black-necked stilt (Himantopus mexicanus), also called the pied stilt, is a locally abundant shorebird of North and South American wetl…

locations.name: 湖泊
locations.summary: 湖泊（lake）是内陆洼地中相对静止、有一定面积，不与海洋发生直接联系的水体。全世界共有约1.17亿个湖泊，共覆盖了地球近500万平方公里。 從地球歷史上來看，湖泊只是暫時存在的水體，會受到泥沙淤積而慢慢陸化；除了少數古老湖泊，如貝加爾湖…

locations.name: 湿地
locations.summary: 濕地（wetland）是永久或季節性被较浅的水（少于6米的淡水、半咸水或海水）掩蓋的獨特自然环境，是一種許多缺氧過程具有優勢的半水生生態系統，也是陸地生態系統和水域生態系統之間的過渡性地帶。有許多水生植物生長在土壤长期被水体浸泡的特定濕地…

locations.name: 中美洲
locations.summary: 中美洲（英語：Central America，西班牙語：América central）是一個地理概念。依據不同的劃分法，屬於北美洲或南美洲的一部分，一般指連接北美洲與南美洲之間的地峽，現有七個國家（若算入墨西哥則為八個）。 範圍 範圍北…

locations.name: 南美洲
locations.summary: 南亚美利加洲（英语：South America），简称南美洲，为七大洲之一，位於西半球南部（或南半球），西臨南太平洋，西面為太平洋板塊及納斯卡板塊。太平洋板塊及納斯卡板塊之間的內營力為張力，衍生了東太平洋海嶺；而納斯卡板塊與南美洲板塊的內…

locations.name: 旧大陆
locations.summary: 旧大陆（拉丁語：Mundus vetus）是指在克里斯托弗·哥伦布发现新大陆之前，欧洲所认识的世界，包括欧洲、亚洲和非洲（全体被称为亚欧非大陆或世界岛）。这个词语是用来與新大陆（包括北美洲，南美洲和大洋洲）相對應。 词源 在考古学和世界历…

relations.subject: Black-necked stilt
relations.evidence: The black-necked stilt is found in estuarine, lacustrine, salt pond and emergent wetland habitats; it is generally a lowland bird but in Central America has been found up to ASL and commonly seen in llanos habitat in northern South America. It is also found in seasonally flooded wetlands

relations.subject: Black-necked stilt
relations.evidence: Its genus is presently monotypic and seems to be closely related to the similar genus Allopyge, found in Old World cranes.Dronen et al

relations.object_summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

relations.subject: Black-necked stilt
relations.evidence: |title=Wetland Birds: Habitat Resources and Conservation Implications |publisher=Cambridge University Press |year=1999 |isbn=0 521 63362 1 |location=United Kingdom |pages=90}} This bird is locally abundant in the San Joaquin Valley, where it commonly winters.McCaskie et al

relations.subject: Black-necked stilt
relations.evidence: Particularly the North American populations of the black-necked stilt have somewhat declined in the 20th century, mainly due to conversion of habitat for human use and pollution affecting both the birds directly as well as their food stocks
relations.object_summary: 农药、工业污染与水体污染影响食物链安全。
